title: Client Particles Particle Effect Component
description: Container for all particle effect components that define behavior and appearance. Components control every aspect of particle effects: emitter shape and rate, particle lifetime and motion, visual appearance and colors, collision responses, and event triggers. Combine components to build complex effects from reusable, modular pieces.

minecraft:particle_initial_speed.title: Particle Initial Speed
minecraft:particle_initial_speed.description: The initial speed of particles. Can be a number, Molang expression, or array of [x, y, z] speeds.

minecraft:particle_expire_if_in_blocks.title: Particle Expire If In Blocks
minecraft:particle_expire_if_in_blocks.description: List of block identifiers. Particles will expire when they are in any of these blocks.

minecraft:particle_expire_if_not_in_bloc.title: Particle Expire If Not In Blocks
minecraft:particle_expire_if_not_in_bloc.description: List of block identifiers. Particles will expire when they are NOT in any of these blocks.

minecraft:particle_kill_plane.title: Particle Kill Plane
minecraft:particle_kill_plane.description: A plane (A, B, C, D coefficients) that kills particles when they cross it. Defined as an array of 4 numbers [A, B, C, D] where Ax + By + Cz + D = 0.

defaultSnippets.label: Emitter Initialization
defaultSnippets.description: Defines Molang expressions that run when the emitter is created and on each update. Useful for initializing and updating emitter variables.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Lifetime Expression
defaultSnippets.description: Controls when the emitter activates and expires using Molang expressions. The activation expression starts particle emission when it evaluates to non-zero, and the expiration expression stops it. This enables dynamic effects tied to entity state—like flames that only appear when an entity is on fire, or magic particles that pulse with the caster's mana.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Lifetime Looping
defaultSnippets.description: Creates repeating particle effects that cycle between active and dormant phases. The emitter runs for the active_time duration, pauses for sleep_time, then repeats indefinitely. Perfect for ambient effects like bubbling cauldrons, flickering torches, pulsing enchantment glows, or creature breathing animations.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Lifetime Once
defaultSnippets.description: Creates a single-burst particle effect that runs once and stops. The emitter activates for the specified active_time duration, emits particles, then terminates. Ideal for impact effects like explosions, block breaking, critical hits, potion splashes, and other momentary visual feedback.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Rate Instant
defaultSnippets.description: Spawns all particles in a single instant burst when the emitter activates. Unlike steady emission that spawns particles over time, this creates an immediate cloud of particles—perfect for explosions, block destruction debris, potion splash impacts, ender teleportation effects, and any effect that needs an instantaneous particle release.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Rate Manual
defaultSnippets.description: Allows manual control over particle emission through events rather than automatic spawning. Particles are only created when explicitly triggered via Molang or particle events, up to the max_particles limit. Useful for effects that should only spawn under specific conditions, like damage indicators, pickup sparkles, or script-controlled visual feedback.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Rate Steady
defaultSnippets.description: Continuously spawns particles at a consistent rate over time. The spawn_rate controls how many particles appear per second, while max_particles limits the total active count. This is the most common emission mode, used for ongoing effects like torch flames, mob auras, weather effects, potion bubbles, and ambient environmental particles.

minecraft:emitter_shape_box.title: Minecraft Emitter Shape Box
minecraft:emitter_shape_box.description:
Properties:
• direction (string)
• offset = [0,0,0] [maxLen: 3]
• half_dimensions [maxLen: 3]
• surface_only (boolean) = false

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Shape Box
defaultSnippets.description: Emits particles from random positions within a rectangular box volume. Define the box using half_dimensions (distance from center to each edge) and offset from the emitter. Particles can emit outward from spawn points, inward toward center, or in custom directions. Ideal for area effects like smoke filling a room, rain in a region, or magic barriers.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Shape Custom
defaultSnippets.description: Emits particles from positions calculated by custom Molang expressions. Unlike preset shapes, this gives complete control over spawn locations using math expressions that can create rings, spirals, waves, or any procedural pattern. Use the offset expressions to define X, Y, Z coordinates relative to the emitter for complex artistic effects.

minecraft:emitter_shape_disc.title: Minecraft Emitter Shape Disc
minecraft:emitter_shape_disc.description:
Properties:
• direction (string)
• offset = [0,0,0] [maxLen: 3]
• radius = 1
• plane_normal (string) = [0,1,0]
• surface_only (boolean) = false

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Shape Disc
defaultSnippets.description: Emits particles from random positions on a flat circular disc. Configure the radius and plane normal to control disc size and orientation. Surface_only restricts spawns to the disc edge for ring effects. Perfect for ground-based effects like summoning circles, shockwaves, splash impacts, and aura rings around entities.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Shape Entity Aabb
defaultSnippets.description: Emits particles from random positions within the attached entity's axis-aligned bounding box. The emission volume automatically scales with entity size, making it perfect for body-covering effects like burning, freezing, poison clouds, healing auras, or damage feedback that should wrap around creatures of any size.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Shape Point
defaultSnippets.description: Emits all particles from a single point in space, offset from the emitter origin. This is the simplest and most common emission shape—all particles spawn at the same location and spread outward based on their initial velocity. Used for torches, candles, sparks, magic projectile cores, and any effect with a focused emission source.

minecraft:emitter_shape_sphere.title: Minecraft Emitter Shape Sphere
minecraft:emitter_shape_sphere.description:
Properties:
• direction (string)
• offset = [0,0,0] [maxLen: 3]
• radius = 1
• surface_only (boolean) = false

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Emitter Shape Sphere
defaultSnippets.description: Emits particles from random positions within a spherical volume. Configure the radius to set sphere size and surface_only to restrict spawns to the outer shell. Direction can be inward, outward, or custom. Ideal for explosions, magical orbs, bubble columns, mob spawn effects, and any effect needing uniform 3D distribution.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Particle Appearance Billboard
defaultSnippets.description: Controls how particle sprites render and orient toward the camera. The facing_camera_mode determines billboard behavior: 'lookat_xyz' always faces the camera (classic sprites), 'rotate_y' only rotates vertically (tall flames), and direction modes align with particle velocity (arrows, sparks). Size controls particle dimensions using Molang for dynamic scaling.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Particle Appearance Tinting
defaultSnippets.description: Applies color tinting to particle textures, from simple solid colors to complex gradients. Use hex colors for static tints, RGBA arrays with Molang for dynamic colors, or gradient objects to blend between colors over particle lifetime. Essential for flames fading orange-to-gray, magical auras shifting colors, or any effect needing color variation.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Particle Initial Spin
defaultSnippets.description: Sets the starting rotation angle and spin velocity for each particle. Initial rotation provides random orientation variety, while spin_rate creates continuous rotation during the particle's lifetime. Use for debris that tumbles, leaves spinning as they fall, sparks that twist, or any particle needing rotational motion.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Particle Initialization
defaultSnippets.description: Runs Molang expressions when each individual particle spawns to initialize per-particle variables. Unlike emitter_initialization which runs once per emitter, this executes for every particle—enabling randomized properties, unique trajectories, or per-particle state that persists through the particle's lifetime.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Particle Lifetime Expression
defaultSnippets.description: Controls individual particle lifetimes using Molang expressions. The max_lifetime sets the absolute duration limit, while expiration_expression can terminate particles early when conditions are met (like leaving a certain area). Enables dynamic particle populations that respond to game state beyond simple time-based expiration.

minecraft:particle_motion_collision.title: Minecraft Particle Motion Collision
minecraft:particle_motion_collision.description:
Properties:
• events
• coefficient_of_restitution (float) = 0
• collision_drag (float) = 0
• collision_radius (float)
• enabled = 1
• expire_on_contact (boolean) = false

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Particle Motion Collision
defaultSnippets.description: Enables particle collision with world geometry. Configure collision radius, bounce coefficient (how much velocity is retained), friction to slow sliding, and whether to expire on impact. Essential for physical particles like rain hitting ground, debris bouncing off surfaces, or arrows embedding in blocks.

defaultSnippets.label: Client Particles Particle Motion Dynamic
defaultSnippets.description: Applies physics simulation to particle movement including gravity, acceleration, and air resistance. Linear acceleration adds constant force (gravity pulls down, wind pushes sideways), while drag slows particles over time. Rotation acceleration and drag control spinning. Creates natural-feeling motion for smoke rising, debris falling, or leaves drifting.
